---
title: "MMP-9 Plots for 2026 FOP GMA Paper"
author: "Natasha Hunt"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

```{r load libraries}
library(ggplot2)
library(ggtext)
library(effsize)
library(readxl)
library(ape)
library(effectsize)
library(ggrepel)
library(ggsignif)
library(R.utils)
library(cowplot)
library(reshape2)
library(dplyr)
library(textshape)
library(readr)
library(readxl)
library(lme4)
library(tidyverse)
library(ggpubr)
```



```{r load data, echo=FALSE}
rm(list = ls())

data <- read_excel("MMP9_data.xlsx")

# Clean NAs from specified columns
data <- data %>%
  filter(!is.na(GENOTYPE) & !is.na(CONDITION) & !is.na(SEX) & !is.na(MMP9))

# Create grouped genotype: combine WT, Pdgfra, and ACVR1 into "Control"
data <- data %>%
  mutate(GENOTYPE_GROUPED = ifelse(GENOTYPE %in% c("WT", "Pdgfra", "ACVR1"), "Control", as.character(GENOTYPE)))

# Factor GENOTYPE with specified levels
data$GENOTYPE <- factor(data$GENOTYPE, levels = c("WT", "FOP", "Pdgfra", "ACVR1"))
data$GENOTYPE_GROUPED <- factor(data$GENOTYPE_GROUPED, levels = c("Control", "FOP"))
data$CONDITION <- factor(data$CONDITION, levels = c("CONTROL", "ABX"))
data$SEX <- factor(data$SEX)
```

```{r shared parameters}
# y and x axis text/titles
yaxis = 20
ytext = 16
xaxis = 16
linesize = 1.5

# genotype colors (updated from treatment_colors)
genotype_colors <- c("WT" = "red", "FOP" = "#00008B", "Pdgfra" = "#ff7f0e", "ACVR1" = "#2ca02c")

# grouped genotype colors
grouped_colors <- c("Control" = "red", "FOP" = "#00008B")

# figure dimensions
width2 = 8
width3 = 9.5
width4 = 11
heightAll = 12

# location for saving figure 
outputfolder = '/Users/nhunt/Desktop/hernandez_lab/projects/2026_MMP9_FOP_Project/r_figures'
dpi = 300
```


```{r boxplot male}
# Filter data for males
data_male <- data %>% filter(SEX == "MALE")

# Create boxplot for males with grouped genotypes
MMP9_male_plot <- ggplot(data_male, aes(x = CONDITION, y = MMP9)) +
  geom_boxplot(coef = Inf, fill = "white", outlier.shape = NA, size = 1.1, show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_jitter(aes(fill = GENOTYPE), position = position_jitter(0.2), size = 3, shape = 21) +
  labs(x = NULL, y = "Serum MMP-9 (ng/mL)", title = "Male") + 
  theme_classic(base_size = 16) + 
  ylim(0,125)+
  theme(axis.line = element_line(size = linesize)) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = genotype_colors, name = "Genotype") +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = c("CONTROL" = "Control", "ABX" = "GMA"))+
  theme(
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = ytext),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = xaxis),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = yaxis),
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = xaxis),
    plot.title = element_text(size = yaxis, hjust = 0.5)
  )

print(MMP9_male_plot)
ggsave(file.path(outputfolder, 'MMP9_Male_Boxplot.tiff'), 
       width = width4, height = heightAll, units = 'cm', dpi = dpi)
```

```{r boxplot female}
# Filter data for females
data_female <- data %>% filter(SEX == "FEMALE")

# Create boxplot for females with grouped genotypes
MMP9_female_plot <- ggplot(data_female, aes(x = CONDITION, y = MMP9)) +
  geom_boxplot(coef = Inf, fill = "white", outlier.shape = NA, size = 1.1, show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_jitter(aes(fill = GENOTYPE), position = position_jitter(0.2), size = 3, shape = 21) +
  labs(x = NULL, y = "Serum MMP-9 (ng/mL)", title = "Female") + 
  theme_classic(base_size = 16) + 
  ylim(0,125)+
  theme(axis.line = element_line(size = linesize)) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = genotype_colors, name = "Genotype") +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = c("CONTROL" = "Control", "ABX" = "GMA"))+
  theme(
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = ytext),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = xaxis),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = yaxis),
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = xaxis),
    plot.title = element_text(size = yaxis, hjust = 0.5)
  )

print(MMP9_female_plot)
ggsave(file.path(outputfolder, 'MMP9_Female_Boxplot.tiff'), 
       width = width4, height = heightAll, units = 'cm', dpi = dpi)

one.wayAnovaMaxLoad <- aov(MMP9 ~GENOTYPE_GROUPED*SEX, data = data)
summary(one.wayAnovaMaxLoad)
TukeyHSD(one.wayAnovaMaxLoad, conf.level = 0.95)
```
```{r boxplot male grouped Control genotype}
# Group WT, Pdgfra, and ACVR1 into the 'Control' group

# Filter data for males
data_male <- data %>% filter(SEX == "MALE")

# Create boxplot for males with grouped genotypes
MMP9_male_plot <- ggplot(data_male, aes(x = CONDITION, y = MMP9)) +
  geom_boxplot(coef = Inf, fill = "white", outlier.shape = NA, size = 1.1, show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_jitter(aes(fill = GENOTYPE_GROUPED), position = position_jitter(0.2), size = 3, shape = 21) +
  labs(x = NULL, y = "Serum MMP-9 (ng/mL)", title = "Male") + 
  theme_classic(base_size = 16) + 
  ylim(0,125)+
  theme(axis.line = element_line(size = linesize)) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = grouped_colors, name = "Genotype") +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = c("CONTROL" = "Control", "ABX" = "GMA"))+
  theme(
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = ytext),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = xaxis),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = yaxis),
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = xaxis),
    plot.title = element_text(size = yaxis, hjust = 0.5)
  )

print(MMP9_male_plot)
ggsave(file.path(outputfolder, 'MMP9_Male_Boxplot_Grouped.tiff'), 
       width = width4, height = heightAll, units = 'cm', dpi = dpi)
```

```{r boxplot female 2}

# Group WT, Pdgfra, and ACVR1 into the 'Control' group

# Filter data for females
data_female <- data %>% filter(SEX == "FEMALE")

# Create boxplot for females with grouped genotypes
MMP9_female_plot <- ggplot(data_female, aes(x = CONDITION, y = MMP9)) +
  geom_boxplot(coef = Inf, fill = "white", outlier.shape = NA, size = 1.1, show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_jitter(aes(fill = GENOTYPE_GROUPED), position = position_jitter(0.2), size = 3, shape = 21) +
  labs(x = NULL, y = "Serum MMP-9 (ng/mL)", title = "Female") + 
  theme_classic(base_size = 16) + 
  ylim(0,125)+
  theme(axis.line = element_line(size = linesize)) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = grouped_colors, name = "Genotype") +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = c("CONTROL" = "Control", "ABX" = "GMA"))+
  theme(
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = ytext),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = xaxis),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = yaxis),
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = xaxis),
    plot.title = element_text(size = yaxis, hjust = 0.5)
  )

print(MMP9_female_plot)
ggsave(file.path(outputfolder, 'MMP9_Female_Boxplot_Grouped.tiff'), 
       width = width4, height = heightAll, units = 'cm', dpi = dpi)


```


```{r boxplot both sexes}
# Group WT, Pdgfra, and ACVR1 into the 'Control' group
# Group males and females

# Create boxplot for males/females with grouped genotypes
MMP9_combo_plot <- ggplot(data, aes(x = CONDITION, y = MMP9)) +
  geom_boxplot(coef = Inf, fill = "white", outlier.shape = NA, size = 1.1, show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_jitter(aes(fill = GENOTYPE_GROUPED), position = position_jitter(0.2), size = 3, shape = 21) +
  labs(x = NULL, y = "Serum MMP-9 (ng/mL)") + 
  theme_classic(base_size = 16) + 
  ylim(0,125)+
  theme(axis.line = element_line(size = linesize)) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = grouped_colors, name = "Genotype") +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = c("CONTROL" = "WT", "ABX" = "GMA"))+
  scale_fill_manual(values = grouped_colors, name = "Genotype", labels = c("Control" = "WT", "FOP" = "FOP"))+
  theme(
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = ytext),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = xaxis),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = yaxis),
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = xaxis),
    plot.title = element_text(size = yaxis, hjust = 0.5)
  )

print(MMP9_combo_plot)
ggsave(file.path(outputfolder, 'MMP9_Boxplot_both_sexes.tiff'), 
       width = width4, height = heightAll, units = 'cm', dpi = dpi)

# stats
CGmodel <- glm(MMP9 ~  (CONDITION*GENOTYPE_GROUPED + (1 | CAGE)), 
              data = data, 
              family = gaussian())  # or appropriate family

summary(CGmodel)

library(lme4)

model <- lmer(MMP9 ~ CONDITION * GENOTYPE_GROUPED + (1 | CAGE),
              data = data)

summary(model)

library(dplyr)
cage_summary <- data %>%
  group_by(CAGE, CONDITION) %>%
  summarise(mean_MMP9 = mean(MMP9), sd_MMP9 = sd(MMP9), n = n(), .groups = "drop")
print(cage_summary)

library(ggplot2)
ggplot(data, aes(x = CAGE, y = MMP9, color = CONDITION)) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.1, height = 0) +
  stat_summary(fun=mean, geom="point", size=3)

```
